text:
Developing a mobile app starts with deciding between native and cross-platform development. Native apps, built for one OS like iOS, offer excellent performance and access to all device features. Cross-platform tools like React Native let you write one codebase for both platforms, saving time and money.

A crucial factor is responsive design, ensuring the app looks good on different screen sizes. After development, apps launch through app stores like Apple’s App Store or Google Play. These stores have strict guidelines and take a percentage of sales. Finally, users expect fast, smooth performance, which means optimizing code and testing thoroughly before release.
